verses.1: A gentle answer turns away wrath, But a harsh word stirs up anger.
verses.2: The tongue of the wise makes knowledge pleasant, But the mouth of fools spouts foolishness.
verses.3: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, Watching the evil and the good.
verses.4: A soothing tongue is a tree of life, But perversion in it crushes the spirit.
verses.5: A fool rejects his father’s discipline, But he who complies with rebuke is sensible.
verses.6: Great wealth is in the house of the righteous, But trouble is in the income of the wicked.
verses.7: The lips of the wise spread knowledge, But the hearts of fools are not so.
verses.8: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, But the prayer of the upright is His delight.
verses.9: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, But He loves the one who pursues righteousness.
verses.10: There is severe punishment for one who abandons the way; One who hates a rebuke will die.
verses.11: Sheol and Abaddon lie open before the LORD, How much more the hearts of mankind!
verses.12: A scoffer does not love one who rebukes him; He will not go to the wise.
verses.13: A joyful heart makes a cheerful face, But when the heart is sad, the spirit is broken.
verses.14: The mind of the intelligent seeks knowledge, But the mouth of fools feeds on foolishness.
verses.15: All the days of the needy are bad, But a cheerful heart has a continual feast.
verses.16: Better is a little with the fear of the LORD Than great treasure, and turmoil with the treasure.
verses.17: Better is a portion of vegetables where there is love, Than a fattened ox served with hatred.
verses.18: A hot-tempered person stirs up strife, But the slow to anger calms a dispute.
verses.19: The way of the lazy one is like a hedge of thorns, But the path of the upright is a highway.
verses.20: A wise son makes a father glad, But a foolish man despises his mother.
verses.21: Foolishness is joy to one who lacks sense, But a person of understanding walks straight.
verses.22: Without consultation, plans are frustrated, But with many counselors they succeed.
verses.23: A person has joy in an apt answer, And how delightful is a timely word!
verses.24: The path of life leads upward for the wise, So that he may keep away from Sheol below.
verses.25: The LORD will tear down the house of the proud, But He will set the boundary of the widow.
verses.26: Evil plans are an abomination to the LORD, But pleasant words are pure.
verses.27: He who profits illicitly troubles his own house, But he who hates bribes will live.
verses.28: The heart of the righteous ponders how to answer, But the mouth of the wicked pours out evil things.
verses.29: The LORD is far from the wicked, But He hears the prayer of the righteous.
verses.30: Bright eyes gladden the heart; Good news refreshes the bones.
verses.31: One whose ear listens to a life-giving rebuke Will stay among the wise.
verses.32: One who neglects discipline rejects himself, But one who listens to a rebuke acquires understanding.
verses.33: The fear of the LORD is the instruction for wisdom, And before honor comes humility.
